title: Have seperate/no UNUSED warning for package parameters
body:
v4.108 introduced UNUSED warnings for parameters (#2627). This unused warning is produced for an unused parameter no matter where it came from. In particular parameters that are imported from a package get an unused warning. A package may contain parameters a particular module or set of RTL doesn't use but are needed elsewhere so you can end up with many UNUSED warnings to waive.

UNUSED does seem more generally useful for module parameters and local parameters where lack of use is more likely to indicate a problem.

I suggest a separate UNUSED warning is created for parameters that come from a package or they don't generate UNUSED warnings at all.

I would be happy to do a PR for this. It appears V3Undriven.cpp is the place to do the change but I haven't managed to work out how to tell if the `AstVar` instance representing the parameter came from an imported package or elsewhere.
